notes:
**Joint release with keen-backend 0.19.1** — multi-pane layout across every primary workspace, Summary section sorting end-to-end, federation Share UI, startup recovery hardening, and reliability fixes.

## Multi-pane layout (opt-in)

Toggle **Panel left** in the toolbar on Tasks, Projects, Knowledge, Summary, or Chat to enable a three-pane layout: a scopes sidebar on the left, a centered list or reading column, and a preview or inspector on the right. The choice persists across all five pages via the same setting (`layout.multiPane`, **off by default**).

- **Tasks** — filter by views, projects, milestones, status, and tags in the sidebar; preview the focused task with in-place status/importance/effort cycling, picker shortcuts, progress notes, and linked entities.
- **Projects & Knowledge** — scope sidebars plus preview panes with inline metrics and linked-item glances (no extra fetches).
- **Summary** — section visibility, calendar source, and filter presets in the rail; task preview when a task is focused.
- **Chat** — conversation list in the left rail (archive/delete per row, New conversation); context chips, pending actions, and session metadata in the right inspector. Pending-action modals are suppressed in multi-pane mode so you aren't double-prompted.

Shared building blocks: `ScopeSidebar` (collapsible sections, refinements header, keyboard nav) and `EntityPreviewPane` (settle-debounced rich preview). Below `lg` / `xl` breakpoints the layout collapses gracefully to the classic single column.

## Summary sections

- **Sort picker** in Settings → Summary Section — choose field (Order, Due date, Priority, Title, Status, Created, Updated) and direction when creating or editing a section.
- **Sort and cap changes now apply immediately** within a session (React Query cache key fix).
- **Backend** — `tasks(filter, sort)` now honors the `sort` argument; Summary sections render in the order you configure instead of always defaulting to due-date ascending.

## Federated collaboration

- **Leader Share UI (R4)** — Settings → Share Project: mint invite URLs, choose permission tier (`read_only` / `edit_status` / `edit_full`), list active shares, and revoke. Tokens live in the URL fragment (never sent over the network). Subscriber **Add** flow accepts a pasted invite URL.
- No schema change in this release — pairs with the Phase 1 backend already shipped in 0.19.0.

## Projects & layout

- **Project status filtering is multi-select** — combine Active, On Hold, Completed, and Archived; Templates is a separate orthogonal view. State persists in the URL and view preferences; legacy `?filter=` bookmarks migrate cleanly.
- **Removed the fixed 1200×1600 window cap** — each page centers content in a comfortable reading column (`~736px` lists, `1000px` detail views) so wide windows show margins instead of over-stretched rows.
- **Project Dashboard** — standalone tasks are now reorderable (drag-and-drop and ⌥↑/⌥↓), matching milestone tasks on the same page.

## Reliability & remote connections

- **Startup auth probe** — a reachable but unusable remote backend (bad token, CORS mismatch) now lands on the recovery screen instead of a broken app shell.
- **Startup connection recovery** — fix server URL, paste a new token, switch to local mode, or clear a stale token directly from the startup error card (no more dead-end "Try again" loop).
- **Test Connection** correctly rejects invalid tokens (GraphQL `UNAUTHENTICATED`, not just HTTP 401).
- **Remote access tokens keyed per server URL** in Keychain — switching servers no longer surfaces a stale token from another host. One-shot migration from the legacy global slot when unambiguous.
- **Chat streaming watchdog** — recovers from missed WebSocket subscription windows (the "thinking" spinner hanging after the agent finished), most visible on Intel Macs and flaky connections.

## Backend (Managed hosting & local server 0.19.1)

- **Summary sort fix** — server-side task ordering for custom Summary sections.
- **LanceDB darwin-x64 cross-compile script** — Intel Mac desktop builds no longer depend on a deprecated npm platform package.

## Install

| Platform | Artifact |
|---|---|
| Apple Silicon | `Keen_0.19.1_aarch64.dmg` |
| Intel Mac | `Keen_0.19.1_x64.dmg` |

Existing installs: **Keen → Check for Updates** or Settings → Updates.
